The genus Zehneria is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the melons or gourds. This species, Zehneria peneyana, is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Pacific Islands. The classification of Zehneria peneyana is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis. As a member of the Cucurbitaceae family, Zehneria peneyana shares similarities with other related species such as the cucumber and melons.
